This chapter contains a general enforcement of all the laws given by Moses; by promises of reward if you obey, on one hand; and threatenings of punishment for disobedience, on the other. While Israel maintained a national regard for God’s worship, sabbaths, and sanctuary, and did not turn aside to idolatry, the Lord promised to continue temporal mercies and religious advantages to them. These great and precious promises, though they relate chiefly to the life you now live, were typical of the spiritual blessings made sure by the covenant of grace to all believers through Christ. 1. Plenty and abundance of the fruits of the earth. Every good and perfect gift must be expected from above, from the Father of lights. 2. Peace under God’s protection. Those who dwell in God dwell in safety. 3. Victory and success in your wars. It is all one with the Lord to save by many or by few. 4. The increase of your people. The gospel church will be fruitful. 5. The favor of God, which is the source of all good. 6. Tokens of his presence in and by his ordinances. The way to have God’s ordinances fixed among you is to cleave closely to them. 7. The grace of the covenant. All covenant blessings are summed up in the covenant relation, I will be your God, and you shall be my people; and they are all grounded upon their redemption. Having purchased them, God would own them and never cast them off until they cast him off.

After God has set the blessing before them which would make them a happy people if they would obey, he here sets the curse before them, the evils which would make them miserable if they were disobedient. Two things would bring ruin. 1. A contempt of God’s commandments. Those who reject the precept will come at last to renounce the covenant. 2. A contempt of his corrections. If you will not learn obedience by the things you suffer, God himself will be against you; and this is the root and cause of all your misery. Also, the whole creation would be at war with you. All of God’s sore judgments would be sent against you. The threatenings here are very particular; they were prophecies, and He who foresaw all their rebellions knew they would prove so. TEMPORAL judgments are threatened. Those who will not be parted from their sins by the commands of God shall be parted from them by judgments. Those wedded to their lusts will have enough of them. SPIRITUAL judgments are threatened, which should seize the mind. They should find no acceptance with God. A guilty conscience would be their continual terror. It is righteous with God to leave those to despair of pardon who presume to sin; and it is owing to free grace if you are not left to pine away in the iniquity you were born in and have lived in.

Among the Israelites, persons were not always prosperous or afflicted according to their obedience or disobedience. But national prosperity was the effect of national obedience, and national judgments were brought on by national wickedness. Israel was under a peculiar covenant. National wickedness will end in the ruin of any people, especially where the word of God and the light of the gospel are enjoyed. Sooner or later, sin will be the ruin as well as the reproach of every people. Oh that, being humbled for your sins, you might avert the rising storm before it bursts upon you! God grant that you may, in this our day, consider the things which belong to your eternal peace.
